title: Configurable shooting training system and method of using the same
abstract: The invention provides a system for pointing targets, at a random sequence, by parameters determined by the device itself or by a human operator. The system includes a main unit and optional remote input/output devices. The system includes a plurality of pointing laser devices that are moveable and rotatable allowing to aim the pointing devices individually. A remote device could be attached or integrated into a firearm.
claims: Claims I Claim: 1. A shooting training system comprising: a control circuit; a user interface connected to said control circuit; at least one sensor connected to said control circuit; a transceiver circuit connected to said control circuit; a power supply circuit; and a plurality of movable aiming elements. 2. The system of claim 1, further comprising a remote unit having: a remote control circuit; a start switch connected to said remote control circuit; a remote transceiver circuit connected to said remote control circuit; and a power supply circuit. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein said user interface includes input and output elements that allow a user to control the system. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein said bypass switches are provided to selectively activate said plurality of aiming elements individually. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein said control circuit activates said plurality of aiming elements based on: a triggering condition sensed by said at least one sensor, software stored on said control circuit, a timer, and user selection. 6. The system of claim 1, wherein said plurality of aiming elements are activated at a predetermined order or at a random order. 7. The system of claim 1, wherein each of the plurality of aiming elements are selectively moved and aimed at a desired location. 8. The system of claim 1, wherein the plurality of aiming elements are provided on a single unit. 9. The system of claim 1, wherein the plurality of aiming elements are provided on different remote units. 10. The system of claim 2, further comprising at least one remote sensor connected to said remote control unit. 11. The system of claim 10, wherein said at least one remote sensor comprises: a piezo-eiectric sensor, a sound sensor, an accelerometer, a vibration sensor or any combination thereof. 12. The system of claim 2, wherein said remote unit is either: attached to a weapon or integrated into said weapon. 13. The system of claim 2, wherein said transceiver circuit communicates with the remote transceiver circuit of said remote unit. 14. The system of claim 2, wherein said plurality of aim ing elements are activated based on a signal received from said remote unit. 15. The system of claim 1, wherein said control circuit controls at least one of: activation speed of said plurality of aiming elements, activation order of the plurality of aiming elements and an amount of aiming elements that are simultaneously activated. 16. The system of claim 1, wherein said plurality of aiming elements comprise a laser. 17. The system of claim 1, wherein said transceiver circuit communicates with an external device so that: said external device controls the activation of said plurality of aiming elements, and the control circuit provides said external device with information related to the operation of the system. 18. The system of claim 1, wherein the control circuit measures at least one of: a time span between shots, a time span between activation of an aiming element and a shot, and a total time for firing a predetermined amount of shots. 19. The system of claim 17, wherein said external device comprises at least one of: a portable phone and a computer, having software that allows remote activation of said plurality of aiming elements and provides a user with information related to the operation of the system. 20. The system of claim 1, wherein said plurality of aiming elements is moved in horizontal and vertical directions.
